skill.name: docs-write
skill.description: Write or edit Metabase documentation using a conversational, clear, and user-focused style guide.
skill.summary: Draft and revise Metabase docs with clear structure, friendly tone, and consistent Markdown formatting.
skill.author: metabase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, SAML wording, Markdown terms, and style-guide prose as high-risk patterns. AI review confirms the SAML, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and Markdown fence hits are false positives. The remaining real concern is broad Bash availability and a Prettier command, which is legitimate for documentation formatting but merits a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Broad Bash Permission for Documentation Formatting
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: SKILL.md declares Bash as an allowed tool and tells the agent to run `yarn prettier --write <file-path>`. This is legitimate formatting guidance, but it permits command execution in a documentation skill and depends on safe file-path handling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool and command are explicit, and the command is for a normal formatter. I found no evidence of exfiltration, persistence, or destructive shell behavior.
medium_findings.remediation: Keep Bash usage limited to repository formatting commands and require the target path to be a documentation file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SAML Text Matched Windows SAM Keyword
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static critical sensitive-data findings point to SAML documentation examples, not the Windows SAM database or credential material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines visibly discuss SAML documentation wording. No credential store, password hash, or Windows database access appears in the file.
low_findings.remediation: No security remediation required for these wording examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Style Terms Matched Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Rules
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The blocker and reconnaissance hits are ordinary documentation guidance, Markdown or MDX references, and wording examples. They do not invoke cryptography, system discovery, or network scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose in a writing style guide. I found no commands, API calls, sockets, cryptographic functions, or host enumeration logic at those locations.
low_findings.remediation: No security remediation required for these style-guide lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fence Examples Matched Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static command-execution hits in the example block are escaped Markdown that demonstrates how to format instructions, not commands the skill asks the agent to execute.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The block is labelled markdown and contains placeholders like command-to-run and next-command. It is instructional formatting content, not executable script code.
low_findings.remediation: No security remediation required for the Markdown formatting example.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Formatter Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends running `yarn prettier --write <file-path>` after documentation edits. This is a normal formatting command, but it is still an external command pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicit and uses a file path placeholder. The context is benign formatting, so the risk is controlled but real.
dangerous_patterns.remediation: Run the command only on intended documentation files inside the repository.

content.value_statement: Technical documentation often becomes formal, vague, or hard to scan. This skill helps draft and edit Metabase docs with clear, conversational guidance.
actual_capabilities: Guides headings to state the main point.
Recommends bold formatting for UI labels and backticks for code.
Suggests running yarn prettier --write after edits.
Flags vague links, peppy tone, and outdated numbers.
limitations: Tailored to Metabase docs, not a generic writing standard.
May use Bash because the skill allows Bash tools.
Does not verify product behavior without source docs or code.
Does not create images or screenshots.

use_cases.title: Revise product documentation
use_cases.description: Turn formal or unclear draft pages into Metabase-style docs with direct headings and useful context.

use_cases.title: Prepare release notes or guides
use_cases.description: Shape feature explanations so readers understand what changed and what to do next.

use_cases.title: Improve developer-authored docs
use_cases.description: Clean up Markdown or MDX files while preserving technical accuracy and command order.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite a short doc section
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this section in the Metabase documentation style. Keep the meaning, improve clarity, and make headings more useful.

prompt_templates.title: Create a task guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a Metabase-style guide for this task. Lead with the action, include needed context, and avoid vague links.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Markdown page
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Markdown page for Metabase style. Identify unclear headings, formal language, weak examples, and formatting problems.

prompt_templates.title: Edit a complex technical article
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this technical article for Metabase docs. Preserve accuracy, improve structure, tighten prose, and list any behavior that needs verification.

output_examples.input: A draft page uses vague headings and says a setup task is simple.
output_examples.output: The revised page uses action-focused headings, removes the word simple, and explains the setup steps in order.

output_examples.input: A link says read the docs here and a UI label is written as inline code.
output_examples.output: The edit replaces the vague link text with a descriptive link and formats the UI label in bold.

output_examples.input: A developer note includes commands inside a paragraph.
output_examples.output: The revised section separates each command into its own step and explains what to do after each command finishes.

best_practices: Provide the audience, product area, and source material before asking for edits.
Ask the agent to preserve technical meaning when rewriting existing documentation.
Review examples and commands manually before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to invent product behavior without evidence.
Do not ask it to run formatter commands on untrusted paths.
Do not treat style edits as a substitute for technical review.

faq.question: What does this skill help write?
faq.answer: It helps write and edit Metabase documentation in Markdown or MDX.

faq.question: Can it format documentation files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill recommends running Prettier after edits, usually with yarn prettier --write.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium warning because it allows Bash for formatting commands.

faq.question: Does it replace a technical review?
faq.answer: No. Product behavior, examples, and commands still need review by someone with domain knowledge.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What style does it enforce?
faq.answer: It favors direct headings, conversational prose, useful context, descriptive links, and accurate examples.
